extraction.one_line_summary: BAUMANN Koen won from breakaway, taking second stage and sealing KOM; GC trio CARAPAZ, HINDLEY, LANDA remained deadlocked at 3 seconds after final climb sparring.
tactical_patterns: Bora-Hansgrohe rode tempo then retreated on final climb with 3-second GC gap, revealing defensive posture when leading by margins under 5 seconds.
12-man breakaway gained 12 minutes before Bora paced, suggesting teams calculated GC threats could be neutralized on 10km final climb.
SIVAKOV pulling through final 3km before CARAPAZ attack shows INEOS using protected rider as final lead-out rather than saving for counter-attacks.
LANDA gapped slightly in final kilometer but CARAPAZ-HINDLEY cooperation closed it, indicating temporary alliances form when leader shows weakness even at 1:05 deficit.

rider_signals.PORT Richie: negative: abandoned with gastroenteritis when climbing started at 62km to go, suggesting illness impacts climbers earlier than flat specialists
rider_signals.SIVAKOV Pavel: positive: strong enough at altitude to pace CARAPAZ through final 3km, indicating genuine climbing form not just domestique duties
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: negative: created small gap in finale but couldn't sustain, suggesting explosive power exists but fatigue prevents capitalizing at 1:05 down
